section13_checks.prohibition_description: Treating Grant Agreement Annex templates as the governing structural schema for proposal writing
Inventing call constraints, scope requirements, expected outcomes, or expected impacts not present in Tier 2B source documents
Inventing project facts — partner names, capabilities, roles, objectives, prior experience, budget figures, team sizes, equipment — not present in Tier 3
section13_checks.violation_evidence: The consistency_log names specific project facts without a formal traceability_footer.primary_sources[] structure: partner identifiers and legal names ('EuroLog International AG', 'Boreal AI Labs Oy', 'ATU', 'BIIS', 'CERIA', 'NIHS', 'ISIA', 'FIIT', 'ELI', 'BAL' — CC-03), objective IDs 'OBJ-1' through 'OBJ-8' (CC-01, CC-06), deliverable ID 'D4-01' (CC-04), work package 'WP9' (CC-01), and KPI identifier 'KPI-03' (CC-06). The artifact contains no 'traceability_footer' field and no 'primary_sources[]' array referencing docs/tier3_project_instantiation/. Inline text references such as 'Tier 3 architecture_inputs/objectives.json' and 'Tier 3 consortium/partners.json' appear within inconsistency_note strings but do not satisfy the formal traceability_footer.primary_sources[] requirement. All project-specific claims in this Tier 5 artifact are therefore structurally unattributed to Tier 3 source files.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Commencing any Phase 8 activity — including preparatory drafting — before the budget gate (Phase 7) has passed
section13_checks.violation_evidence: The artifact schema_id 'orch.tier5.part_b_assembled_draft.v1' begins with 'orch.tier5.' classifying this as a Phase 8 artifact under Check 13.4. Compliance requires confirming that docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase7_budget_gate/budget_gate_assessment.json exists with gate_pass_declaration: 'pass'. That file is outside the declared TAPM input boundary for this invocation and cannot be read. Budget gate passage cannot be confirmed from available inputs. Per CLAUDE.md §15, the system must prefer explicit gate failure over fabricated completion; the gate is therefore treated as unconfirmed-presumed-violation. CC-08 in the consistency_log shows internal budget consistency ('consistent', no inconsistency_note) across all three sections, but internal consistency of references does not constitute confirmation that the external Phase 7 gate was evaluated and passed before Phase 8 assembly commenced.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Storing durable decisions only in agent memory without writing them to Tier 4
section13_checks.violation_evidence: Not applicable at artifact-level audit: this prohibition governs agent runtime behaviour (decisions held in agent memory vs. written to Tier 4) and cannot be verified by inspecting the content of a deliverable artifact. Recorded as pass per skill specification Step 2 instruction for 13.5.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Allowing a workflow or skill to become a de facto constitutional authority by expanding its scope to redefine phase meanings, tier meanings, or gate logic
section13_checks.violation_evidence: Not applicable at artifact-level audit: this prohibition governs workflow and skill scope creep and cannot be evidenced by reading a Tier 5 deliverable artifact. Recorded as pass per skill specification Step 2 instruction for 13.6.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Silently reordering phases or weakening gate conditions to allow workflow progress when inputs are incomplete
Finalizing proposal text when source state is known to be incomplete, contradictory, or unvalidated, without explicitly flagging the gap in the deliverable
section13_checks.violation_evidence: The artifact explicitly flags five internal inconsistencies in its consistency_log rather than silently suppressing them: CC-01 (objective count discrepancy: 7 enumerated vs 8 defined in Tier 3, status 'inconsistency_flagged'), CC-03 (partner legal-name truncations for ELI and BAL, status 'inconsistency_flagged'), CC-04 (D4-01 purpose description and due month both inconsistent across Excellence and Impact sections — 'Both cannot be correct simultaneously. This inconsistency must be resolved before final submission.', status 'inconsistency_flagged'), CC-06 (KPI omission: ≥15% delivery schedule adherence target absent from Impact section KPI register, status 'inconsistency_flagged'), CC-09 (terminology inconsistency: 'External Tool and API Orchestration Layer' vs 'external tool and API orchestration capability', status 'inconsistency_flagged'). All five are documented with explicit inconsistency_notes. The artifact does not contain a validation_status.overall_status field (it is an assembled draft index, not a proposal section), but the inconsistencies are explicitly recorded rather than hidden.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Using agent-local knowledge of Horizon Europe programme rules as a substitute for reading Tier 1 source documents when those documents are present and accessible
Producing outputs in Tier 5 that are not traceable to specific inputs in Tier 1–4
section13_checks.violation_evidence: The artifact entirely lacks a 'traceability_footer' field. No 'traceability_footer.no_unsupported_claims_declaration' is present, and no 'validation_status.claim_statuses[]' array exists to support the formal traceability check. The consistency_log entries reference project-specific facts — including partner legal names ('EuroLog International AG', 'Boreal AI Labs Oy'), objective IDs ('OBJ-6', 'OBJ-7', 'OBJ-8'), deliverable ID 'D4-01', work package 'WP9', and KPI 'KPI-03' — without structured Tier 1–4 source attribution. Inline text citations within inconsistency_note strings (e.g., 'Tier 3 architecture_inputs/objectives.json OBJ-6.measurable_target', 'Tier 3 consortium/partners.json') are not a substitute for the formal traceability_footer.primary_sources[] structure required of Tier 5 artifacts by CLAUDE.md §11.4 and §10.5. The assembled draft is structurally non-compliant with the Tier 5 traceability requirement.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Modifying Tier 1 or Tier 2 source documents to reflect project-specific assumptions
section13_checks.violation_evidence: Not applicable at artifact-level audit: this prohibition governs write operations to Tier 1 and Tier 2 source directories and cannot be evidenced by reading a Tier 5 deliverable artifact. Recorded as pass per skill specification Step 2 instruction for 13.11.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Treating CLAUDE.md as advisory or optional
section13_checks.violation_evidence: Not applicable at artifact-level audit: this prohibition governs agent and skill execution posture toward the constitution and cannot be directly evidenced by reading a Tier 5 deliverable artifact. Recorded as pass per skill specification Step 2 instruction for 13.12.

decision_log_entries.resolution_note: Constitutional violation detected; requires human operator resolution. The assembled draft names project-specific partners, objectives, deliverables, WPs, and KPIs in its consistency_log without a formal traceability_footer.primary_sources[] structure referencing docs/tier3_project_instantiation/. The artifact must be amended to include a formal traceability_footer that maps each project-specific claim to its Tier 3 source file, or the consistency_log must be restructured so that each inconsistency_note carries a structured source_ref rather than inline text.

Constitutional violation detected; requires human operator resolution. This Phase 8 artifact (schema_id orch.tier5.part_b_assembled_draft.v1) cannot be confirmed to have been produced after the budget gate (Phase 7) passed. The human operator must confirm that docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase7_budget_gate/budget_gate_assessment.json exists with gate_pass_declaration: 'pass' and that this gate was evaluated before the assembled draft was created. If the budget gate was not passed, all Phase 8 outputs must be rescinded per CLAUDE.md §8.4 and §13.4.

Constitutional violation detected; requires human operator resolution. The assembled draft lacks the traceability_footer structure required of all Tier 5 artifacts per CLAUDE.md §11.4 and §10.5. The artifact must be amended to include a traceability_footer with primary_sources[] entries for each material claim, a no_unsupported_claims_declaration field, and a validation_status block. The schema_id orch.tier5.part_b_assembled_draft.v1 must be updated to include these mandatory traceability fields if they are absent from the registered schema, or the artifact must be regenerated against a compliant schema version.
